name: Trial of spontaneous breathing
description: Spontaneous breathing trial (SBT) — test of ventilator-weaning readiness; the act of performing the trial.

en.name: Trial of spontaneous breathing
en.shortDescription: Spontaneous breathing trial (SBT) — test of ventilator-weaning readiness; the act of performing the trial.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

A spontaneous breathing trial (SBT) is a test in which a mechanically ventilated patient is allowed to breathe with little or no ventilator support for a short period, to assess whether they are ready to be weaned off the ventilator and extubated. In source data this is a **procedure** record marking that an SBT was performed; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care as part of the weaning process. The trial may be done on a T-piece, on low-level pressure support, or on CPAP, but the concept here captures the act of performing the trial regardless of how it is delivered.

## Included Concepts

This set contains the single SNOMED concept for a spontaneous breathing trial:

- *4308797 / 424139009 — Trial of spontaneous breathing (SNOMED)*: a spontaneous breathing trial. Synonym: "Spontaneous breathing trial".

## Mapping Notes

- Map a recorded spontaneous breathing trial (SBT) to *4308797 / 424139009 — Trial of spontaneous breathing*, regardless of the technique used to deliver it (T-piece, pressure support, CPAP).
- **SBT vs weaning**: this set is the trial itself, not the broader weaning process. The act of weaning from ventilation (reducing/withdrawing support) is a distinct concept and is not included here.
- **SBT vs extubation**: the trial assesses readiness; the actual removal of the tube is captured in the Extubation concept set.
